title: What Does Being Older Bring?
subtitle: A journal practice for examining what age has given, not just taken
excerpt: A journal practice for examining the gains of ageing alongside the losses. Asks what being older has brought in terms of clarity, relationships, and the things that no longer need managing. For the moment when the cultural narrative only counts losses.

practice.whenToUse: Use when ageing feels like only a subtractive process, or when you have been counting what you have lost without counting what you have gained.

content.text: A twenty-minute journal practice for examining what age has brought as well as what it has taken. The cultural account of ageing is largely subtractive. This practice asks for the other side of the ledger.
What do you know now that you did not know at thirty?
Write the practical knowledge, not the abstract wisdom. What you know about money, people, your own body, how things actually work.
What things no longer need managing that used to take a lot of energy?
The social anxiety, the approval-seeking, the things you dressed up for or performed for. Write what has quietened down.
Which relationships are better now than they were twenty years ago?
Name them specifically. What is better about them.
What are you less afraid of than you used to be?
The specific fears. Conflict, failure, what people think, certain kinds of social situations. Write what has eased.
